concept: Assemble a platformer where levels exist on the surface of small planetoids with localized gravity pulling toward each planetoid's core, allowing full 360-degree movement around each spherical environment. Jumping between planetoids creates zero-gravity transitions requiring precise trajectory control, with different sized planetoids offering varying gravitational strengths that affect movement mechanics. Environmental hazards include gravitational anomalies that temporarily reverse or redirect gravitational pull, requiring quick adaptation to suddenly changed movement physics. Advanced techniques involve using momentum gained from one planetoid's gravity well to execute perfect orbital transfers between distant objectives. The ultimate challenge features a complex planetary system with constantly shifting orbital patterns, requiring perfect understanding of gravitational interactions to navigate successfully.
